chrome.errorFallbackMessage: Something went wrong loading the page. Try reloading.

landing.headline: IQ-ME
landing.intro: IQ-ME is a fluid-reasoning screener that uses 16 visual matrix-reasoning items (ICAR-MR), takes about 25 minutes, runs entirely in your browser, and stores nothing on a server. It is not a clinical assessment, a credential, or a placement test — it is one calibrated screen of one narrow ability.

consent.headline: Before you begin
consent.measuresWhat: This instrument measures fluid reasoning — your ability to identify visual-spatial patterns under no time pressure. It does not measure verbal reasoning, processing speed, working memory in isolation, or any clinical condition.
consent.validityEnvelope: IQ-ME is valid as a screener for fluid reasoning in adults who can complete a 16-item visual session unimpaired by acute distress, sleep deprivation, or substances. It is not valid as a diagnostic, as a measure under time pressure, or as a stand-in for a multi-domain IQ battery.
consent.visuospatialDisclosure: The matrix items are visual by construction. Screen-reader users cannot take this test equivalently — there is no synthetic alt-text that captures the patterns honestly. We disclose this rather than pretend otherwise.

itemRunner.optionsLegend: Select the option that best completes the pattern
itemRunner.fetchErrorMessage: Could not load the item set. Try reloading.
itemRunner.bailExplanation: An incomplete test cannot produce a meaningful score.

result.prerevealSubcopy: It is one estimate with a range around it.
result.caveat: This screener is not a clinical assessment, not a credential, not an educational-placement tool, and not a basis for employment or legal decisions.
result.difficultySentenceTemplate: Of the {hardN} hard, {medN} medium, and {easyN} easy items, you answered {hardCorrect} / {hardN}, {medCorrect} / {medN}, and {easyCorrect} / {easyN} correctly.
result.fetchErrorMessage: Could not load the item set. Try reloading.
result.retestNote: This screener draws from a small pool of matrix items. If you take it again soon, your second score will be correlated with this one rather than an independent estimate — you will partly be remembering these patterns, not re-measuring your reasoning from scratch. We enforce no technical cooldown: that would be a false guarantee in a browser-only app. We simply ask you to read this and decide honestly whether a quick retest tells you anything new.

validityEnvelope.title: IQ-ME validity envelope
validityEnvelope.desc: Three population zones labelled valid for, partial validity, and not valid for. The diagram shows IQ-ME applies as a screener of fluid reasoning in adults who can complete a 16-item visual session unimpaired; partial validity for users with mild fatigue or transient distraction; not valid for clinical diagnosis, time-pressured assessment, or stand-in multi-domain IQ batteries.

eapShrinkage.title: EAP shrinkage at the tails
eapShrinkage.desc: An EAP point estimate sits between a raw maximum-likelihood theta and the standard-normal prior centre at theta zero. At the tails, the prior pulls the estimate toward zero — visible shrinkage. Near the centre, the prior has little effect.
